commit.message:
Update tsdown (#2652)

* fix(plugin-client): skip invalid JS variable names in path param const declarations

Path parameters with dashes (e.g. organization-id) produced invalid
JavaScript like `const organization-id = organizationId`. The URL
template already converts these to camelCase, so the const declaration
is unnecessary. Filter out entries where the original name is not a
valid JavaScript identifier.

Fixes #2465

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* test(plugin-client): add tests for path params with dashes (#2465)

Add dashedPathParams and dashedPathParamsInline test cases using an
OpenAPI spec with `organization-id` path parameter to verify that
dashed parameter names produce valid JavaScript output.

* feat: migrate to Azure AI Foundry + Content Understanding with unified server logic

BREAKING CHANGES:
- Bicep output names changed: AZURE_OPENAI_* → AI_FOUNDRY_*
- Environment variables renamed: AZURE_OPENAI_ENDPOINT → AI_FOUNDRY_ENDPOINT, etc.
- Search index schema changed: location_metadata fields renamed
- Separate OpenAI + AIServices resources consolidated into single AIServices resource

## Infrastructure (Bicep)

- Consolidate two Cognitive Services resources (OpenAI + AIServices) into a single
  AIServices resource (`kind: AIServices`) with model deployments (embeddings, chat)
- Remove `aiFoundryServices` module; `aiFoundry` now serves both model hosting and
  skillset billing via AIServicesByIdentity
- Rename all Bicep parameters, modules, role assignments, and outputs from
  `openAi`/`AZURE_OPENAI_*` to `aiFoundry`/`AI_FOUNDRY_*`
- Update resource naming: `cog-*` prefix → `aifs-*` prefix
- Remove `AI_FOUNDRY_SERVICES_ENDPOINT` and `AI_FOUNDRY_SERVICES_SUBDOMAIN_URL`
  outputs; replaced by `AI_FOUNDRY_SUBDOMAIN_URL`
- Update cognitiveservices.bicep metadata description

## Search Pipeline (Content Understanding)

- Replace `DocumentIntelligenceLayoutSkill` with `ContentUnderstandingSkill`
  for structure-aware extraction with built-in chunking
- Remove `TextSplitSkill` — chunking now handled by Content Understanding
  `chunkingProperties` (unit: characters, maximumLength: 2000, overlapLength: 200)
- Update index schema: `location_metadata` fields changed from
  `page_number`/`bounding_polygons` to `pageNumberFrom`/`pageNumberTo`/
  `ordinalPosition`/`source` to support chunks spanning page boundaries
- Simplify index projections: use direct `locationMetadata` source mapping
  instead of nested `sourceContext` + `inputs` blocks
- Update embedding skill context from `/document/text_sections/*/chunks/*`
  to `/document/text_sections/*` (no more sub-chunks)
- Rename vectorizer from `openai-vectorizer` to `ai-foundry-vectorizer`
- Update API version from `2025-05-01-Preview` to `2025-11-01-Preview`
  across all pipeline scripts (.sh, .ps1, .http)
- Update all `resourceUri` references from OpenAI endpoint to AI Foundry endpoint

## Server (src/server.ts)

- Extract shared `performSearch()` and `performFetch()` functions — single source
  of truth for search and fetch logic across REST, MCP, and legacy endpoints
- Add `buildPageFilter()` for OData range-based page filtering
  (pageNumberFrom <= P AND pageNumberTo >= P)
- Add `fetchAllDocuments()` with server-side ordering by pageNumberFrom, ordinalPosition
- Implement text/image chunk separation in `FetchResult`:
  - `text`: deduplicated continuous text (text chunks only)
  - `images`: array of `ImageDetail` objects (image chunks only)
  - `textChunks`/`imageChunks`: counts by type
  - `chunkDetails`: all chunks with type classification
- Add 200-char overlap deduplication for Content Understanding chunks
- Add `formatFetchAsText()` — structured markdown with Text Content (page headers)
  and Images (bullet list) sections
- Add `formatSearchAsText()` — structured markdown with metadata per result
- Add `pageLabel()` helper for page range formatting
- Update `SearchDocument` interface: location_metadata uses new field names
- Add type interfaces: SearchResultItem, ChunkDetail, ImageDetail, FetchResult

## Deployment Scripts

- Update deploy.sh/deploy.ps1: rename output variable extraction from
  `openai_name`/`openai_endpoint` to `ai_foundry_name`/`ai_foundry_endpoint`
- Update deploy output display: Azure OpenAI → Azure AI Foundry,
  fix embeddings model name in display (text-embedding-ada-002 → text-embedding-3-large)
- Update cleanup.sh/cleanup.ps1: rename purge messages and comments
- Update validate-deployment.sh/validate-deployment.ps1: rename resource checks

## Documentation

commit.message:
fix(state): remove fatal assertionFailure for detached nodes (#10)

`@State` getter/setter crash with `assertionFailure` when accessed after
the view's `Node` is deallocated. This is fatal in debug builds. Common
trigger: an async `.task(id:)` closure sets `@State` after the view is
removed from the hierarchy — the `weak var node` in `StateReference`
becomes `nil`, hitting the assertion before the guard's fallback path.

```
State.wrappedValue.setter + 688
  assertionFailure("Attempting to modify @State variable before view is instantiated")
```

The message is also misleading — this isn't "before instantiation," it's
after removal.

### Changes

- **`State.swift`**: Remove `assertionFailure` from both getter and
setter guards. The fallback behavior (return `initialValue` / no-op) is
already correct and matches SwiftUI semantics.
- **`StateTests.swift`**: Direct unit tests for `State` get/set when
`valueReference.node` is nil.
- **`LifecycleModifierTests.swift`**: Integration test verifying a
`.task` closure can complete after its view is removed without crashing.

<details>

<summary>Original prompt</summary>

> 
> ----
> 
> *This section details on the original issue you should resolve*
> 
> <issue_title>Feature: Expose Prometheus metrics on a dedicated
internal port</issue_title>
> <issue_description>### Background
> Following the OpenTelemetry instrumentation (see previous issue), we
need a way to actually scrape and monitor these metrics in production.
Prometheus is the industry standard for this.
> However, exposing a `/metrics` endpoint on the main proxy port is
dangerous, as it risks hijacking legitimate upstream paths and exposing
sensitive internal metrics to the public internet.
> 
> ### Proposal
> Configure the OpenTelemetry Prometheus exporter and expose the
`/metrics` endpoint on a **completely separate, dedicated internal HTTP
port**. This metrics server should only start if explicitly enabled via
configuration.
> 
> ### Configuration
> - `MANAEL_METRICS_PORT`: The port on which to expose the metrics
server (e.g., `9090`). If not set or empty, the metrics server is
disabled.
> 
> ### Tasks
> - Add dependencies for `go.opentelemetry.io/otel/exporters/prometheus`
and `github.com/prometheus/client_golang/prometheus/promhttp`.
> - Configure the OpenTelemetry MeterProvider to use the Prometheus
exporter.
> - If `MANAEL_METRICS_PORT` is set, start a new background goroutine
with an HTTP server listening on that port, serving `promhttp.Handler()`
at the `/metrics` path.
> 
> ### Example Implementation Idea
> ```go
> package main
> 
> import (
> 	"log"
> 	"net/http"
> 	"os"
> 	"go.opentelemetry.io/otel/exporters/prometheus"
> 	"go.opentelemetry.io/otel/sdk/metric"
> 	"github.com/prometheus/client_golang/prometheus/promhttp"
> )
> 
> func setupMetricsServer() {
> 	metricsPort := os.Getenv("MANAEL_METRICS_PORT")
> 	if metricsPort == "" {
> 		return // Metrics server disabled
> 	}
> 
> 	// Setup Prometheus exporter
> 	exporter, err := prometheus.New()
> 	if err != nil {
> 		log.Fatalf("failed to initialize prometheus exporter: %v", err)
> 	}
> 	provider := metric.NewMeterProvider(metric.WithReader(exporter))
> 	// Set global MeterProvider...
> 
> 	go func() {
> 		metricsMux := http.NewServeMux()
> 		metricsMux.Handle("/metrics", promhttp.Handler())
> 		log.Printf("Starting metrics server on :%s", metricsPort)
> if err := http.ListenAndServe(":"+metricsPort, metricsMux); err != nil
{
> 			log.Printf("Metrics server error: %v", err)
> 		}
> 	}()
> }
> ```</issue_description>

Fix workspace tree: SSE updates must merge, not replace

The workspace-update SSE handler was replacing the entire tree with
the broadcast root (depth=2), wiping out all previously-loaded subtrees.
Now uses mergeTree() so expanded subtrees are preserved when the server
broadcasts a shallow workspace update.

> <issue_title>Feature: Add a dedicated health check endpoint
(/_health)</issue_title>
> <issue_description>### Background
> Currently, Manael acts as a catch-all reverse proxy, forwarding all
incoming requests to the upstream server. When deployed to container
orchestrators like Kubernetes or Google Cloud Run, it is best practice
to provide a dedicated health check endpoint (Liveness/Readiness probe)
that returns a `200 OK` directly from Manael without putting load on the
upstream server.
> 
> ### Proposal
> Add a lightweight, dedicated health check endpoint at `/_health`. 
> Using the `_` prefix minimizes the risk of colliding with legitimate
upstream application paths. To further improve security and prevent
external probing, we can optionally restrict this endpoint so that it
only responds to requests coming from localhost/loopback or specific
internal IP ranges.
> 
> ### Example Implementation
> ```go
> func main() {
> 	mux := http.NewServeMux()
> 	
> 	// Dedicated health check endpoint
> mux.HandleFunc("/_health", func(w http.ResponseWriter, r
*http.Request) {
> 		// Optional: Restrict to localhost or private IPs if necessary
> 		// if !isLocalhost(r.RemoteAddr) { ... }
> 		
> 		w.WriteHeader(http.StatusOK)
> 		w.Write([]byte("OK"))
> 	})
> 
> 	// Register the proxy for all other routes
> 	proxyHandler := manael.NewServeProxy(upstreamURL, options)
> 	mux.Handle("/", proxyHandler)
> 
> 	srv := &http.Server{
> 		Addr:    conf.httpAddr,
> 		Handler: mux,
> 	}
> 	// ...
> }
> ```</issue_description>
